food_subgroup: Pizza — topped flatbread with cheese, cured meat, and egg

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic pizza class (FOODON_00002572). No specific code exists for maple-glazed bacon pizza with tater tots and egg. Closest parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A00LC covers pizza as a category. No specific facet code exists for this topped variant with maple glaze, bacon, tater tots, and soft egg. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226789007 is the generic pizza concept. No specific descendant exists for this restaurant variant.

serving.natural_language_portion: One individual pizza of approximately 350 g, as typically served at a restaurant, comprising a baked dough base topped with fior di latte, smoked bacon, maple glaze, smashed tater tots, and a soft egg.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Pizza with meat, NS as to type of crust (SR Legacy 21299) and component ingredient data for bacon, maple syrup, potato, egg, and mozzarella
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact USDA or HPB entry exists for maple glazed bacon pizza with tater tots and soft egg. Values are estimated by weighted-ingredient composition using USDA SR Legacy data for: pizza dough/crust (USDA 18413), fior di latte / part-skim mozzarella (USDA 01029), smoked bacon (USDA 10124), maple syrup (USDA 19353), potato (tater tots, USDA 11371 / 11674), and whole egg (USDA 01123). Proportions estimated as: crust 30%, mozzarella 20%, bacon 18%, tater tots 15%, egg 10%, maple glaze 7%. Per-100g values derived from weighted blend of these components.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Pizza, cheese, thin crust (GI 57, glucose reference)
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for maple glazed bacon pizza with tater tots. The closest analogous food in the University of Sydney GI Database is cheese pizza on thin crust (GI 57, glucose scale), published in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). The maple glaze adds simple sugars which may slightly elevate GI; however, the dominant carbohydrate source remains the pizza crust. GI 57 is used as the best defensible analogous estimate. Glycemic load per serving = (57 × 82.25 g carbohydrate in 350 g serving) / 100 = 46.9.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content from smoked bacon, cheese, and processed dough
hypertension.guidance: At approximately 520 mg sodium per 100 g (estimated 1,820 mg per individual pizza serving), this dish substantially exceeds the recommended 600 mg sodium per meal threshold for hypertensive individuals and should be consumed only occasionally with careful portion control.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Medium glycemic index (GI 57), high refined carbohydrate load, and added maple glaze sugar
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic load of approximately 46.9 per serving and added simple sugars from the maple glaze, this pizza poses a significant postprandial glucose challenge for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes and should be limited to small portions paired with non-starchy vegetables.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from bacon and mozzarella, high sodium, and cholesterol from egg and bacon
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of saturated fat (approximately 5.1 g per 100 g), high sodium (~520 mg per 100 g), and dietary cholesterol (~72 mg per 100 g) makes this dish a high-concern food for individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease, and regular consumption is not recommended.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Maple Glazed Bacon Pizza is an Italian-style pizza served at Mingle restaurant in Funan, Singapore. The pizza features a baked dough base topped with fior di latte (fresh cow's milk mozzarella), smoked bacon, a homemade maple glaze, smashed tater tots, and a soft-cooked egg. The flavour profile combines savoury, smoky, and sweet notes — the maple glaze imparts a caramelised sweetness that contrasts with the saltiness of the smoked bacon and the mild creaminess of the fior di latte. The smashed tater tots add a crispy, starchy texture element unusual in traditional Italian pizza. Nutritionally, this is a calorie-dense, high-sodium dish with moderate protein from the bacon, cheese, and egg. The saturated fat content from the bacon and cheese, combined with the high sodium from cured meat and processed dough, makes this a high-concern food for individuals managing hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or Type 2 Diabetes. The glycemic index is estimated at 57 (medium), with a high glycemic load per serving due to the large portion size and added maple syrup sugars. This dish is not halal due to the pork bacon content. It contains gluten (dough), dairy (mozzarella), and egg allergens. Priced at SGD 32.50, it is positioned as a premium restaurant pizza in the Singapore dining market.

confidence_reason: No exact match found in any approved primary or secondary nutrient database for this specific restaurant pizza. Nutrient values are estimated from a weighted blend of USDA SR Legacy component ingredient data. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food (cheese pizza, thin crust, GI 57) in the University of Sydney GI Database. Confidence is low due to the composite estimation approach and the absence of direct laboratory analysis or an approved database entry for this dish.
notes: 1. Food group corrected from input 'Grains and staples' to 'Mixed' as this is a composite pizza dish with multiple food groups (dough, dairy, meat, egg). 2. Cuisine confirmed as Italian based on pizza format and fior di latte ingredient, consistent with input cuisine tags. 3. This dish contains pork (smoked bacon) and is therefore not halal. 4. Multilingual local names for Chinese, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi have been added to comply with the G6 rule for dishes served in Singapore, despite the dish being Italian cuisine. These names are descriptive translations of 'Maple Glazed Bacon Pizza' as no established local names exist for this specific fusion dish. 5. Nutrient values are estimated composites — monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are all estimated from USDA SR Legacy component data and should be treated as approximate. 6. Glycemic load per serving calculated as (GI 57 × carbohydrate per serving 82.25 g) / 100 = 46.9; carbohydrate per serving = 23.5 g/100g × 350 g serving = 82.25 g. 7. Price SGD 32.50 preserved from input source (field survey data). 8. No -1 sentinel values are used for core macros; all secondary nutrient -1 values have been replaced with estimated numeric values from USDA component data, so no -1 sentinel documentation is required.

improvement_actions_taken: Populated local_names for Chinese (simplified), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi with descriptive translations of 'Maple Glazed Bacon Pizza' to comply with the G6 multilingual naming compliance rule for dishes served in Singapore.
Added the new local-language names and their romanized form to the flat 'aliases' list and the 'aliases_by_language' object to ensure consistency and full coverage.
Updated the 'notes' field to reflect the addition of these multilingual names and the rationale for their inclusion based on validator feedback.
Updated veritas_qc.criteria.q1_food_identity.notes and veritas_qc.criteria.q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_coverage.notes to reflect the completed multilingual requirements.
Updated veritas_meta.iteration_count_used to 2 and generated_at timestamp.
